item.title: Taiko halts its Ethereum layer-2 network after an attacker exploited the bridge to steal $1.7M via forged cross-chain proofs.
Humanity Protocol lost $32M after an attacker used private keys stored on one employee laptop to drain its Ethereum and BNB bridges.
North Korea-linked actors accounted for 76% of global crypto hack losses through the first four months of 2026, up from 64% in 2025.
JPMorgan is exploring institutional loans collateralized by Bitcoin and Ethereum holdings, per a Financial Times report.
BlackRock has amended its Coinbase custody agreement to require 12-hour withdrawal notice windows for its crypto fund assets.
CME Group filed suit against the CFTC challenging the regulator's approval of Kalshi's Bitcoin perpetual futures contracts.
The SEC's draft five-year strategic plan designates digital assets and blockchain as a standalone regulatory priority for the first time.
CFTC names Donald Battle, formerly of the SEC's crypto task force, as chief data innovation officer focused on blockchain forensics.
Malta's financial regulator proposes bringing centralized elements of DeFi protocols under the EU's MiCA regulatory framework.
BIS warns that crypto exchanges offering lending and yield products are functioning as lightly regulated shadow banks.
Morgan Stanley and Charles Schwab are both moving to launch direct cryptocurrency trading services for their client bases.
US Bancorp has resumed institutional Bitcoin custody services, extending coverage to support spot Bitcoin ETF operations.
Microsoft identified a USB-spread worm that intercepts wallet shortcut files to harvest private keys from cryptocurrency wallets.
BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC have captured the dominant share of net inflows among all U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs in 2026.
